name: Lara de los Infantes from Los Ausines - circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route
name_original: Lara de los Infantes desde Los Ausines — circular por el Camino del Cid Cicloturista
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Los Ausines traverse a landscape characterized by its rural setting, offering wide, open views across grassy fields and access to distant mountains. The region features a mix of historical sites and natural beauty, including converted railway lines known as Vías Verdes. These routes often include mostly paved surfaces, catering to various fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Los Ausines The most popular touring cycling route is Lara de los Infantes from Los Ausines - circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route, a 33.4 miles (53.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Camino del Cid. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Arch of Santa María – Burgos loop from Carcedo de Burgos, a moderate 15.0 miles (24.2 km) path. This route offers views of the Arch of Santa María, a significant landmark in Burgos. Local touring cyclists also love the Burgos Cathedral – Arch of Santa María loop from Modúbar de la Emparedada, a 74.5 miles (119.8 km) trail leading through the wider region, often completed in about 7 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Los Ausines is defined by open rural landscapes, distant mountain views, and accessible Vías Verdes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Los Ausines are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Ausines's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes are available around Los Ausines?
A: Los Ausines offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, from moderate to difficult, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find routes traversing open rural landscapes, offering distant mountain views, and utilizing accessible Vías Verdes (greenways). The terrain often features mostly paved surfaces.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Los Ausines?
A: Yes, Los Ausines has options suitable for families and those seeking easier rides. The region is known for its Vías Verdes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5694583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterráneo Greenway (Burgos Section)&lt;/a&gt;, which are converted railway lines offering flat, paved paths. These greenways are ideal for a relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see while cycling near Los Ausines?
A: While cycling around Los Ausines, you can explore several natural highlights. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3849773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carcedo Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7328949" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The routes themselves often provide wide, open views across grassy fields and glimpses of distant mountains.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region around Los Ausines is rich in history. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2052933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Cardeña&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious building with Romanesque and Gothic elements. Some routes also lead to landmarks like the Arch of Santa María in Burgos or follow sections of the historic Camino del Cid.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available from Los Ausines?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027753303/lara-de-los-infantes-from-los-ausines-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lara de los Infantes from Los Ausines - circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 33.4-mile (53.7 km) trail.

Q: What is the general condition of the cycling paths in Los Ausines?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Los Ausines feature mostly paved surfaces, making them accessible for various skill levels and types of touring bikes. The Vías Verdes, in particular, are well-maintained converted railway lines offering smooth, flat paths.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Los Ausines?
A: The touring cycling routes in Los Ausines are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the accessible Vías Verdes, and the combination of natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: Are there any unique features on the Vías Verdes near Los Ausines?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Burgos section) offers a unique experience, including a tunnel of approximately 500 meters. Cyclists should be prepared with a headlamp for passage through this section, adding an adventurous element to the ride. You can find this tunnel highlighted as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4152122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel on the Vía Verde Santander–Burgos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is a good moderate touring cycling route to explore the area?
A: For a moderate ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arch of Santa María – Burgos loop from Carcedo de Burgos&lt;/a&gt;. This 15.0-mile (24.2 km) path offers scenic views and takes you towards the significant Arch of Santa María in Burgos.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced touring cyclists looking for a challenge can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Cathedral – Arch of Santa María loop from Modúbar de la Emparedada&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult trail spans 74.5 miles (119.8 km) and leads through the wider region, often taking over 7 hours to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Los Ausines?
A: Los Ausines is a promising destination for outdoor activities, including touring cycling, throughout much of the year. The region's diverse routes and mostly paved surfaces make it accessible for different skill levels. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the generally pleasant climate of the Burgos province suggests spring and autumn offer comfortable temperatures for cycling.

Q: Are there any other interesting places to visit near Los Ausines besides cycling?
A: Beyond cycling, the rural setting of Los Ausines provides a great base to explore nearby attractions. You can visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5458997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Doña Sancha&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Cubillejo de Lara&lt;/a&gt;. The broader region also offers charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5709149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hontoria de la Cantera&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Lara de los Infantes is a small town in the province of Burgos. It has a Romanesque church, the Church of Our Lady of the Nativity, and, on top of a hill, are the ruins of a medieval castle. Legend has it that Fernán González, the first independent count of Castile, was born and grew up in this castle.

It is a funerary megalithic monument of the corridor tomb type, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1985. It is approximately 5,000 years old. It is made up of a circular chamber about five meters in diameter and a wide corridor about ten meters long.

The town of Los Ausines make up the Barrio de San Juan, the Barrio de Sopeña and the Barrio de Quintanilla. Among the places of interest it is worth mentioning: the hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Castillo, the Church of Santa Eulalia and a beautiful recreational area next to it and the Castromayor Site, which preserves a wall from the II Iron Age (the wall preserves about 70 centimeters high and 3 meters wide).
